items.briefing:
A new type of ransomware called "JadePuffer" is making headlines as the first fully AI-driven ransomware attack, meaning cybercriminals can now automate and accelerate attacks with less effort than before. Separately, a vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler — a product used by many Australian organisations for remote access — is being actively exploited, so if your workplace uses this technology, IT teams should prioritise patching immediately.

Closer to home, research shows many Australian workers are using AI tools on the job without telling their employers, which can expose sensitive organisational data to unintended risks. **Practical tip:** If you use AI tools for work tasks, check with your IT or management team first to ensure the tool is approved and safe.

featured.title: Data centre influx applies pressure on AU emissions targets
featured.quote: Data centre expansion threatens Australia's net zero emissions targets amid billions in infrastructure investment.
